subject: Chemistry
title: Melanin: The Color of Skin and Hair
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Melanin: The Color of Skin and Hair.

hook.text: One molecule controls human skin color, hair color, and eye color: melanin. More melanin means darker color.

buildup.text: Eumelanin produces brown and black. Pheomelanin produces red and yellow. Your mix determines your coloring.

discovery.text: Melanin absorbs UV radiation, protecting DNA from sun damage. Darker skin evolved near the equator for UV defense.

twist.text: Gray hair isn't gray—it's transparent. Hair loses melanin with age, and light scattering makes it look silver.

climax.text: Albinism results from mutations that prevent melanin production. Without it, skin and eyes have no UV protection.

punchline.text: One pigment family, every shade of human. Melanin is evolution's sunscreen.

quiz.question: Why did darker skin evolve in equatorial regions?
options: More melanin absorbs UV radiation, protecting DNA from sun damage
Darker skin retains more heat in cold nights
Melanin repels tropical insects more effectively
